steps.title: Identify Text 2's explanation for why results differ between studies
steps.content: Text 2's author explains why Créte and Jolicoeur's moose study found no predation effect while other predation relief studies show prey increases: those studies typically examine small, rapidly reproducing prey like mice, while the moose study involves large, slowly reproducing prey. The difference in outcomes hinges on prey characteristics.

steps.title: Match the author's logic to the choices
steps.content: Choice A captures the key distinction Text 2's author makes: Risbey's mouse findings result from small, rapidly reproducing prey characteristics that moose lack. Choices B and C introduce predator size ratios and comparisons between feral cats and wolves, neither of which Text 2 discusses; D contradicts Text 2's explicit statement that many predation relief studies show similar increases in various small prey. That matches choice A — A is correct.
